messages.content: Howard Schultz ………... Chief Executive by the company's Board.
Howard Schultz was appointed as the Chief Executive by the company's Board.
Babies ............when they are hungry.
Babies cry when they are hungry.
She has a very clear vision for the long-term future of the company, but many people think she is too approachable. Is it True or False?

Based on the given information, it is unclear whether the statement is true or false. The statement describes that "many people think she is too approachable," but it does not specify who the person in question is.
I mean is it grammatically True or False?

The statement "She has a very clear vision for the long-term future of the company, but many people think she is too approachable" is grammatically true. There are no apparent errors in grammar in this statement.
If the selection panel members consider that your application has sufficient merit, you will be contacted and invited to ..........
If the selection panel members consider that your application has sufficient merit, you will be contacted and invited to proceed to the next stage of the selection process.
I ……….. take some exercise if I want to feel fine.
I should take some exercise if I want to feel fine.
You………...touch anything electrical if you are in the bath.
You should not touch anything electrical if you are in the bath.
In some countries, talking about the weather for a minute or two is a way to …………
In some countries, talking about the weather for a minute or two is a way to start or initiate a conversation.
International markets for labour, capital, goods and services exploit poverty in poorer countries. Is it grammatically True or False?
The statement "International markets for labour, capital, goods, and services exploit poverty in poorer countries" is grammatically true. There are no apparent errors in grammar in this statement.
International markets for labour, capital, goods and services promote higher ............ of living, working conditions, technology, education, etc.'
International markets for labour, capital, goods, and services promote higher standards of living, working conditions, technology, education, etc.
These days, many applicants submit their………. speculatively to companies they would like to work for.
These days, many applicants submit their resumes speculatively to companies they would like to work for.
You ……….. get a visa to enter the country.
You need to get a visa to enter the country.
A………….is a part of a company's profits paid to the owners of shares.
A dividend is a part of a company's profits paid to the owners of shares.
An anti-· ............ directive was passed to prevent the proceeds of organised crime circulating through the financial system.
An anti-money laundering directive was passed to prevent the proceeds of organized crime circulating through the financial system.
I ...........to bed very late last night.
I went to bed very late last night.
…………. there any cars on the street?
Are there any cars on the street?
Dieter Zetsche (become) Head of Mercedes in September 2005.
Dieter Zetsche became the Head of Mercedes in September 2005.
I (travel) from Australia to Europe in September.
I will travel from Australia to Europe in September.
Someone who is …………. is friendly and easy to talk to.
Someone who is approachable is friendly and easy to talk to.
I ..............tennis yesterday.
I played tennis yesterday.
I saw Kate a few minutes ago. She--------- for you.
I saw Kate a few minutes ago. She is looking for you.
Banks were recently accused of…………, as apparently female staff are often paid 40% less than their male counterparts.
Banks were recently accused of gender pay discrimination, as apparently female staff are often paid 40% less than their male counterparts.
Toledano travelled every week to one of Dior's 224 stores. Is it grammatically True or False ?
The statement "Toledano travelled every week to one of Dior's 224 stores" is grammatically true. There are no apparent errors in grammar in this statement.
Brand loyalty is how familiar people are with a brand (or its logo and slogan). Is it grammatically True or False ?

The statement "Brand loyalty is how familiar people are with a brand (or its logo and slogan)" is grammatically false. 

Brand loyalty refers to the extent to which consumers consistently choose a particular brand over others in the market, based on their positive perception and experiences with that brand. It is not solely dependent on familiarity with the brand's logo and slogan.
A fashion designer who launches his or her own perfume is an example of brand……….
A fashion designer who launches his or her own perfume is an example of brand extension.
He which has never learned to obey cannot be a good commander. Is it grammatically True or False?
The statement "He who has never learned to obey cannot be a good commander" is grammatically true. There are no apparent errors in grammar in this statement.
With our market dominance in Asia, we're in an extremely powerful position. We are really in the …………….
With our market dominance in Asia, we're in an extremely powerful position. We are really in the driver's seat.
A contract worth 200,000 dollars. The grammatically correct sentence is?
The contract is worth 200,000 dollars.
A 200,000-dollar contract Or A 200,000-dollars contract or 200,000-dollars contract
The grammatically correct sentence would be "A 200,000-dollar contract."
Starbucks (start) by Howard Schultz
Starbucks was started by Howard Schultz.
We are willing to supply the goods………a discount of 5%.
We are willing to supply the goods at a discount of 5%.
